subject: Geography
source: Liberia Ministry of Education National Curriculum

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to relate life on the earth to its planetary position in space and determine time difference in relation to the earth rotation.
objectives: Describe the formation of the universe
Describe the solar system in relation to its formation
Draw a diagram of the solar system to show the planets in orbit around the sun
contents: The formation and composition of the universe
The formation and structure of the solar system
The planets
The moon and its impact
activities: Learners draw the solar system and explain its structure and formation
In a group, learners demonstrate the concept of the moon's rotation and revolution by walking around a chair with one side of the body always facing the chair
Learners draw the various phases of the moon as it revolves around the earth
Use flash light and football to demonstrate the causes of day and night
materials: General Geography in Diagrams for West Africa

topics.topic: Shape and Size of the Earth
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to elaborate the concepts of Diameters and Circumferences and prove the shape of the earth.
objectives: Explain the concepts of Equatorial Circumference, Equatorial Diameter, Polar Circumference, and Polar Diameter
Measure the Equatorial circumference, Polar circumference, Polar diameter, and Equatorial diameter
Prove the shape of the earth by calculating the differences between the polar and equatorial diameters and the circumferences
contents: Earth's Dimension: Equatorial circumference, Equatorial diameter, Polar circumference, Polar diameter
Differences in length between equatorial and polar circumference
Differences in length between equatorial diameter and polar diameter
Shape of the Earth: Description
Proof of the Earth's spherical shape: inferential proof, aerial photographs, circumnavigation of the earth, lunar eclipse, earth's curved horizon, apparent sunrise/sunset, Bedford canal experiment
activities: Using a string, ruler and orange or egg, learners in a small group measure Equatorial circumference, Polar circumference, Polar diameter, and Equatorial diameter
As an exercise, learners plant three poles of equal height on a level ground and observe the height of the center pole to prove the spherical shape of the earth
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to demonstrate understanding of latitudes and longitudes.
objectives: Relate the terms latitudes and longitudes
Discuss the major latitudes and longitudes
Explain the uses of longitudes and latitudes
contents: Lines of Latitude: definition, major lines, characteristics, uses, linear distance calculation
Lines of Longitude: definition, major lines, characteristics, uses
Great and small circles
Local time calculation
Graticules
North and South Poles, True North, Magnetic North, Grid North, Magnetic declination
activities: Learners work in small groups to identify and show the importance of longitude and latitude
Learners outline major latitude and longitude lines on the globe
Using Venn diagrams, learners identify similarities and differences between lines of latitude and longitude
Using Venn diagram, learners identify similarities and differences between Great Circles
Calculate time in different time zones
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Wall map with lines of latitude and longitude

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to distinguish the concepts of earth rotation and revolution as well as apply the Ferrell's law of deflection.
objectives: Differentiate between the concepts of rotation and revolution of the earth
Explain the terms associated with the rotation and revolution of the earth
Describe the effects of the earth's rotation and revolution
Discuss the deflection of winds and ocean currents using Ferrell's law
Distinguish between lunar eclipse and solar eclipse
Describe the formation of solar eclipse and lunar eclipse
contents: The Rotation of the Earth: definition, effects of rotation
The Revolution of the Earth: definition, terms associated with revolution, effects of revolution
Similarities and differences between rotation and revolution
Eclipse: definition, formation, types (solar and lunar)
activities: Learners carry out experiment by pointing flash light at a football to demonstrate the appearance of day and night
Learners spin the globe or football to indicate movement of the earth
Learners draw a simplified diagram of the earth showing the movement of winds to the north and south of the equator
Learners illustrate solar and lunar eclipse by diagrams
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.topic: The External and Internal Structure of the Earth
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss features of the earth's surface.
objectives: Distinguish between the external and internal layers of the earth
Discuss the features of the geologic layers of the earth's surface
Describe the characteristics of the external and internal layers of the earth
contents: External Structure (major spheres): Atmosphere, Hydrosphere, Biosphere, Lithosphere
Characteristics of the spheres
Importance of the spheres to living organisms
Relationship within and among the spheres
Internal Structure layers: Crust, Mantle, Core
Characteristics of the layers
activities: Draw a simplified diagram of the earth's internal and external structure to show the different types of layers
Field trip to identify external and internal layers of the earth
Use clay to mold the internal structure of the earth
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to differentiate between rocks and minerals, outline the characteristics of rocks and minerals and state the economic importance of rocks.
objectives: Distinguish between rocks and minerals
Classify rocks in relation to mode of formation
List the main characteristics and uses of each type of rock
contents: Types of rocks: Igneous, Metamorphic, Sedimentary
Characteristics of rock: texture, structure, color, permeability
Characteristics and mode of formation: Igneous rocks, Metamorphic rocks, Sedimentary rocks
Uses of Igneous Rocks, Metamorphic Rocks, Sedimentary Rocks
Importance of rocks to man
activities: Group discussion: learners collect different types of rocks from the community/school and classify different samples of rock types
Group discussion on rock texture, hardness and color of rock pieces/samples; the mode of formation and uses
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.unit: Human and Regional Geography – World Population and Settlement
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to explain world population and distribution patterns; recognize factors that influence population movements, the importance of family planning and empowerment as well as population census.
objectives: Discuss the term 'World population'
Explain population distribution patterns of the world
Distinguish between overpopulation and under population
Explain the factors that influence population movements
Demonstrate the conduct of population census
Recognize the importance of family planning and empowerment as a means of controlling population growth
contents: World Population: definition, characteristics, size
Terms associated with population growth
Factors affecting population growth
Reasons for rapid growth of world population
Pattern of world population distribution: densely populated areas, moderately populated areas, sparsely populated areas
Advantages and disadvantages of high and low population densities
Population control and family planning
activities: Draw population maps indicating densely and sparsely populated regions
List some overpopulated and underpopulated counties/countries
Hold debates to discuss population control in relation to family planning and women empowerment
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Pictures and diagrams of places where population data can be collected
Video clips of densely and sparsely populated areas
Illustrative pictures of controlled and uncontrolled population

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to distinguish between rural and urban settlements, outline the consequences of migration and suggest ways to minimize migration.
objectives: Classify settlement according to types, pattern, size, and function
Analyze the factors affecting siting and growth of settlement
Explain the functions of settlements
Describe the characteristics of settlement according to types, pattern, and function
Explain the consequences of migration at the source and receiving regions
Advance possible solutions to migration
contents: Settlement: definition, site and situation, factors affecting siting and growth
Classification by type: Urban and Rural
Classification by pattern: nucleated, linear, dispersed, isolated
Classification by function: commercial, religious, administrative, industrial
Classification by size: Cities, Towns, Metropolis, Satellite towns
Classification of villages: Homestead, Hamlet, Farmstead
Characteristics and functions of rural and urban settlements
Reasons for patterns of settlement
Urbanization: definition, causes, problems, solutions
Migration: definition, forms and types, causes (push and pull factors), effects, controls
activities: Learners discuss factors affecting settlement growth, and draw diagrams of settlements
List the contribution of rural and urban settlements to mankind
Learners work in groups to outline the consequences of migration at the source and receiving regions
Learners work in groups to identify problems associated with migration and suggest solutions
Field trip to identify types of settlements, over and underpopulated regions of Liberia
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Illustrative diagrams of settlements according to pattern
Reports of International Organization of Migration (IOM)

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to interpret map information and convert scales; design diagrams and determine positions of places.
objectives: Interpret map information
Distinguish kinds of maps
Convert from one scale to another
Apply skills to reduce, enlarge, and calculate areas and ground distances of maps using map scales
Calculate the gradient of slopes
Discover the positions of places using the grid system, or longitudes and latitudes
Determine directions using the compass and bearing
Design diagrams, profiles and sketches resulting from survey exercises
contents: Map: definition, characteristics, types, importance/uses, limitations
Map Scale: definition, types (Representative Fraction, Statement, Linear)
Advantages and disadvantages of each type
Conversion of Map Scales
activities: Learners outline and discuss the kinds of map and indicate their features
Learners draw map to scale, reduce and enlarge maps and state the limitations of maps
Draw the prismatic compass and illustrate its direction and bearing
Prepare an annotated map using the three types of map scales
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to elaborate on the various forms of land and water masses, their importance to mankind; explain the major environmental hazards that result from natural and human activities.
objectives: Determine the ratio of land to water
Determine how much land is suitable for agriculture
Identify the various forms of water masses
Discuss the importance of land and water masses to man
Describe the major environment
Identify the major components of the environment
Discuss the importance of the environment
Identify the problems facing the environment
contents: The continents
The islands
Vegetation resources
Mineral resources
Case study: Mineral resources of Liberia, West Africa and other parts of Africa
Water Resources: Oceans, Seas, Rivers, Lakes, Rias
Environment: definition, types, components, domains, importance, problems
activities: Learners diagram the earth's surface to identify land and water positions, providing the ratio of land and water
Group learners to discuss the relevance of the atmosphere to living organisms and challenges posed by the atmosphere
Brainstorm and discuss the continents and oceans of the world and show their locations using maps
Field trip to identify variations in vegetation and mineral types
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Geological maps of Liberia, West Africa and Africa
Illustrative diagrams and pictures of water bodies
Video clips/tapes of continent and island

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to identify the different types of pollution, their effects on the ecosystem, and create awareness for control measures.
objectives: Elaborate on pollution and its kinds
State the effects of pollution on the environment
Suggest ways through which pollution can be minimized and controlled
Discuss land ecosystem
contents: Land Pollution: sources, effects, prevention/control
Water Pollution: sources (river contamination, DDT use, industrial waste), effects
Land Ecosystem: meaning and components, food chain, land reclamation, nitrogen cycle, problems and solutions
activities: Learners work in groups to identify sources of land and water pollution in the environment
Group discussion and presentation on the ways of controlling land and water pollution
Outline the chemicals responsible for the contamination of water and land
Class discussion/assignment: Define land ecosystem and name the components, identifying the elements that make up the ecosystem
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to identify the different types of soil, state their importance to the growth and development of plants and animals to mankind.
objectives: Classify the different soil types based on their characteristics
Discuss the factors involved in soil formation
Examine the influences on soil fertility
Elaborate on the importance of soil to human and animal life
contents: Soil types and characteristics
Soil formation
Processes of soil formation: humidification, mineralization
Soil fertility
Importance of soil: plant life, provision of minerals, source of raw materials, base for construction, habitat for fauna and flora
activities: Group learners to identify the different soil types and their characteristics using their school environment
Draw soil profile and label the layers
Group discussion on the formation of soil and the importance for agricultural and construction activities
Learners outline different soil types in Liberia
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Illustrative diagrams and pictures of soil profiles

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to outline the kinds of environmental hazards and propose prevention mechanisms.
objectives: List the different kinds of environmental hazards
Propose ways to avoid or manage environmental hazards
Explain the effects of environmental hazards on human activities
contents: Environmental Hazards: meaning, types, causes, effects and control
Soil Erosion and Depletion
Drought
Deforestation, global warming, desertification: meaning, causes, loss of farmlands, aridity, change in pattern of agriculture, prevention/control
Flooding: meaning, causes (precipitation, melting of ice), effects (displacement of population, loss of life and property), prevention and control
activities: Learners identify and discuss incidences of environmental hazards locally and elsewhere
Group discussions on the causes and effects of environmental hazards
Using poster sheets, learners list and describe the different kinds of environmental hazards; outline preventive methods to constructively control hazards
Field trip to identify areas affected by environmental hazards
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Illustrative pictures and diagrams of logging, flooding, pollution, desert landscape, greenhouse effect/global warming

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to classify landforms as they relate to the formation of mountains, plateaus and plains.
objectives: Describe landforms
Classify landforms in relation to structure
State the importance of landforms
Summarize the formation of mountains, plateaus and plains
contents: Landforms: Mountains, Plateaus, Plains
Types of mountains: Fold mountain, Volcanic mountain, Block mountain, Residual mountain
Characteristics and formation of the four types of mountains
Economic importance of the four types of mountains
Advantages and disadvantages of mountains
Plateaus: types, characteristics, formation, advantages and disadvantages
Plains: types, characteristics, advantages and disadvantages
activities: Class exercise: Diagram the four types of mountains
Group discussion on the importance of the four types of mountains
Field trip to a mountain to show the base and peak, and differences between mountains, plateau and plains
Illustrate fold mountain formation by compressing two sides of a paper
Learners shake and open a bottle of Coca-Cola to demonstrate the formation of volcanic mountains
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to demonstrate knowledge of the hydrosphere in relation to its importance and discuss the water cycle.
objectives: Identify the components of the hydrosphere
Highlight the importance of hydrosphere to life on planet Earth
Relate the hydrosphere to other natural features of the earth
Analyze the water cycle
contents: Water cycle: definition, processes
Definition and components of the hydrosphere: Oceans, Seas, Rivers, Lakes
Diagram and analysis of the water cycle
Importance of hydrosphere
activities: Group discussion on the meaning and the components of the hydrosphere
Field trip to identify the various water bodies that constitute the hydrosphere
State the importance of hydrosphere to life on planet earth
Diagram the water cycle
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.unit: Physical Geography – Internal Structure of the Earth
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss the lithosphere as the land block of the earth with emphasis on its external and internal portions, and its importance to life on earth.
objectives: Classify the internal and external composition of the Lithosphere
Outline the different land blocks (the continents)
Compare the lithosphere to other natural features of the earth
contents: Definition and composition of lithosphere
Land blocks (continents)
Internal composition: Sial and Sima
External composition (continents)
Map of the continents
Diagram of the internal and external compositions
Importance of the lithosphere
activities: Group discussion on the meaning and the composition of the lithosphere
Field trip outside the school compound to observe the land mass and differentiate lithosphere from other layers
State the importance of lithosphere to life on planet earth
Learners draw diagram to illustrate the internal and external parts of the lithosphere
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Illustrative diagram of the sial and sima

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to elaborate on the biosphere and its importance to man, plants and animals.
objectives: Explain the Biosphere and its composition
Discuss the importance of Biosphere as life layer of the earth
Identify the various biochores in the biosphere
Compare and contrast the biosphere to other natural features of the earth
contents: Definition and composition of biosphere: Forest, Swamps, Deserts
Identification and discussion on the various biochores in the biosphere
Importance of biosphere as life layer of the earth
Diagram of the composition of the biosphere
Differentiation of aquatic bio cycle from terrestrial bio cycle in the biosphere
activities: Learners brainstorm on the components of the biosphere using poster sheets
Group discussion on the composition of the biosphere
Field trip outside the school compound to observe different plants and animals that make up the biosphere
Draw the aquatic and the terrestrial bio cycle in the biosphere
Learners draw Venn diagram to show the position of the biosphere in relation to other spheres
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to effectively describe faulting and folding; demonstrate knowledge in natural internal geomorphic forces moulding landforms.
objectives: Discuss faulting and folding as forces that produce landforms
Differentiate between faulting and folding in relation to land formation
Identify the major landforms resulting from faulting and folding
Explain plate tectonics and the theory of continental drift
Analyze the proofs of the theory of continental drift
contents: Plate tectonics: definition, oceanic and continental plates, plate boundaries, effects of plate tectonics
The theory of continental drift: definition, evidence/proofs (minerals, rock, coastal)
Types of folding: simple fold, asymmetrical fold, recumbent fold
Types of faulting: normal fault, reverse fault, tear fault
activities: Field trip: Organize field trips to observe various landforms in Liberia and make sketches
Learners mould clay to illustrate vertical and lateral earth movements
Stretch rubber bands to demonstrate faulting
Compress face towel to illustrate folding
Learners trace continents, cut and join them together to prove the theory of continental drift
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.unit: Physical Geography – Internal Forces Producing Landforms
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to explain vulcanicity and identify features produced as the result of vulcanicity; demonstrate knowledge of the positive and negative impact of natural phenomena.
objectives: Explain the term 'Vulcanicity'
Identify the causes of vulcanicity
Describe the features produced by volcanic action
Discuss the effects of vulcanicity and earthquake
contents: Vulcanicity: definition and terms, causes
Intrusive features: Batholiths, Dykes, Sills, Laccoliths, Geysers
Extrusive features: Composite cone, Ash and cinder cone, Lava plateau, Lava plain, Crater, Caldera
Effects of vulcanicity and earthquake: tourist attraction, mineral deposits, fertile soils, pollution
Effects of earthquake: death, displacement of people, destruction of property
activities: Group discussion on ways vulcanicity and earthquake affect the population; identify affected regions; economic importance of volcanic landforms
Classwork/assignment: Draw diagrams indicating the formation of volcanic features
Learners shake a bottle of Coca-Cola and open it to demonstrate volcanic eruption
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Illustrative diagrams and pictures of volcanic action

topics.outcomes: Learners will acquire the knowledge and skills of minimizing the negative impact of the agents modifying landforms.
objectives: Distinguish between weathering and mass wasting
Outline the factors affecting weathering and mass wasting/movement
Discuss the types of weathering and mass wasting
Analyze the effects of weathering and mass wasting
contents: Denudation: definition, factors affecting denudation, sequences of denudation
Weathering: definition, factors affecting weathering
Types of weathering: Physical, Biological, Chemical
Processes involved in the three types of weathering
Effects of weathering
Mass wasting/mass movement: definition, factors affecting, types, effects
activities: Learners identify regions of the world where the types of weathering are very peculiar
Learners go on a field trip to observe soil creeps, landslide and rock fall
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Illustrative diagrams and pictures of mass wasting

topics.unit: Physical Geography – External Processes Modifying Landforms
topics.topic: Action of Running Water (Rivers), Winds and Waves
topics.outcomes: Learners adopt means of minimizing river, wind and wave erosion.
objectives: Describe the processes of river, wind and wave erosion
Identify landforms produced by river, wind and wave
Analyze the destructive and constructive actions of river, wind and wave
Describe the mode and formation of landforms produced by river, wind and wave
contents: Action of running water (river): factors affecting velocity, development of river valley, processes of erosion, processes of transportation, landforms by erosion, landforms by deposition, importance of rivers
Action of winds: characteristics of deserts, types of desert landscape, processes of wind erosion, processes of wind transportation, landforms by wind erosion, landforms by wind deposition
Action of waves: development of wave, processes of wave erosion, processes of wave transportation, landforms by wave erosion, landforms by wave deposition
Types of coast: submergence coastline, emergence coastline
activities: Organize field trip to observe the impacts of river, wind and wave actions
Illustrate river profile by diagrams
Identify the different types of coastlines in Africa
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Illustrative diagrams and pictures of landforms produced by rivers, wind and wave

topics.unit: Physical Geography – Internal Process of Landform Development
topics.outcomes: Learners will appreciate the importance of underground water to man; develop strategies to harness vulcanicity to the benefit of man.
objectives: Describe the processes that cause the development of the hydrological cycle
Explain the hydrological cycle
Define underground water
Explain the terms associated with underground water
Discuss the mode of formation of surface and underground limestone features
contents: Hydrological cycle: definition, terms (overland flow, infiltration, through flow, evaporation, condensation), importance
Ground water: definition, terms (zone of permanent saturation, springs, wells, artesian basin), importance of underground water
Features produced by groundwater: stalagmites, stalactites, pillars
activities: Learners illustrate hydrological cycle through drawing
Collect samples and identify permeable and impermeable rocks
Conduct experiment to practicalize water table
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Diagrams and pictures of hydrological cycle

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to elaborate on the atmosphere, its characteristics and usefulness.
objectives: Elaborate on the Atmosphere and its composition
Outline the layers of the atmosphere and their characteristics
Discuss the usefulness of the atmosphere
Identify atmospheric pollution
List methods of protecting the atmosphere
contents: Atmosphere: definition, characteristics and composition, importance, problems and solutions
activities: Class exercise: List the composition and layers of the atmosphere using poster sheets
Group discussion on the meaning and usefulness of the atmosphere
Group work: Research on the causes of atmospheric pollution and ways to minimize pollution
Draw the atmosphere showing the different layers
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.topic: Atmospheric Pressure and Air Pollution in Liberia
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to explain atmospheric pressure and air pollution in Liberia.
objectives: Explain atmospheric pressure
Discuss the types of wind on the basis of temperature variation
Explain air pollution
Outline the causes and impact of air pollution in Liberia
contents: Atmospheric Pressure: definition, temperature variation (wind direction/speed, pressure belts, zones of convergence/divergence)
Planetary wind system
Deflection of winds
Land and sea breezes
Warm and cool/cold air
Currents, shape of coastline
Air pollution: definition, causes, effects, control
Case study: Air pollution in Liberia
activities: Class work: Draw a barometer on poster sheet and explain its usage
Home work: Outline the different types of winds on the basis of temperature variation
Group work: Learners research and report on some causes of air pollution in Liberia
Group discussion/Brainstorm on the control of air pollution, outline the causes of wind
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.topic: Effects of Weather and Climatic Elements; Climatic Classification and Climatic Regions
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to differentiate between weather and climate and analyze climatic data.
objectives: Differentiate between Weather and Climate
List the elements of weather and climate
Calculate and analyze climatic data using charts
contents: Weather: definition, elements, weather station, weather record (temperature, humidity, rainfall, atmospheric pressure), attributes, importance
Climate: definition, differences between weather and climate, factors affecting climate, elements of climate, climatograph, importance
Classification of climate: Greeks and Koppen's classifications, basis, advantages and disadvantages
Climatic regions: Equatorial, Tropical, Mediterranean, Hot and cold deserts, Warm temperate, Cool temperate, Cold temperate, Polar
activities: Class Discussion: group learners to discuss the causes of weather and climatic changes; state the importance of climate
Group presentation on the weather instruments and their uses
Class exercise: Prepare a climatic chart and calculate mean annual temperature, mean monthly temperature, range of rainfall and temperature
Outdoor observation to feel changes in the weather condition
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Pictures and diagrams of weather instruments

topics.topic: Natural Vegetation and Human Economic Activity
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss vegetation and the related economic activities in Liberia.
objectives: Discuss vegetation
List the different types of natural vegetation
Summarize the factors that affect vegetation
Outline the economic activities in Liberia that are related to the natural vegetation
contents: Natural Vegetation: definition and types
Development of vegetation: climatic, biotic, soil
Human Economic Activities: primary, secondary, problems and solutions
Case Study: Liberia (Forest, Mountain, Savanna, Mangrove Swamps and Marshlands)
activities: Class Discussion/Brainstorm: Define vegetation and relate it to climate; Name human economic activities that affect natural vegetation
Field trip with learners to show the various species of plants
Home work: Outline the various types of natural and cultivated vegetation; research and report on deforestation, afforestation and reforestation
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss agriculture and systems of farming as well as outline factors affecting agriculture.
objectives: Outline primary industries
Identify factors affecting the development of primary industries
Discuss agriculture as a primary industry
Classify agriculture according to type
Compare the systems of farming
Explain the factors that affect agriculture and their importance
contents: Definition of Primary Industries
Major Primary Industries
Agriculture classification: Subsistence and Commercial, Crop and Animal Farming
Systems of farming: Shifting cultivation, Bush fallowing, Mechanized farming, Crop rotation, Truck farming, Pastoralism, Nomadism, Dairy farming
Problems affecting agriculture
activities: Class discussion: Learners define agriculture and relate it to primary industry
Group work and debate on mechanized and subsistence farming in Liberia (boys vs girls)
Field trip for learners to observe farming sites
Homework: Distinguish between intensive and extensive farming; outline farming methods
Learners research and report on why agriculture is a primary industry
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Illustrative pictures and diagrams of subsistence and commercial farming
Data and statistics of world agricultural produce

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to explain fishing and its importance as well as outline conditions that favor fishing.
objectives: Locate the major fishing grounds in the world
List the local and international methods of fishing
Explain conditions that favor fishing
Discuss the importance of fishing
contents: Major Fishing Grounds: The Grand Banks, The coast of Peru, The Sea of Japan
Methods of Fishing: Hook and line, The use of nets, The use of wicker baskets
Conditions that favor fishing: Meeting of warm and cool ocean currents, Abundance of plankton
Importance of fishing, problems and solutions
Case study: Fishing in West Africa
activities: Class discussion: Define fishing and relate it to primary industry; State importance to development; Outline conditions favoring fishing in Liberia
Home work: Outline the major fishing grounds in the World
Group work and presentation on the methods of fishing (boys vs girls)
Field trip to observe major breeding sites and fishing grounds in and around the community
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Pictures and diagrams of major world fishing

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss mining and relate the importance of mining to national development.
objectives: Explain mining
Classify the different types of minerals
Identify the major mineral producing countries of the world
Outline the uses of major minerals in the world
Analyze the importance of mining to national economies
Discuss problems facing the mining industry in Liberia and other West African states
contents: Mining: definition, types of minerals (Metallic: Ferrous and Non-Ferrous; Non-Metallic; Mineral Fuels)
Uses of selected minerals
Methods of Mining: Open Pit/Open Cast, Adit, Shaft/Underground, Dredging, Drilling
Importance of the Mining Industry: infrastructural development
Problems and solutions
Case Studies: Iron ore mining in Liberia, Gold mining in Ghana and South Africa, Oil mining in Delta Region of Nigeria, Copper mining in Katanga Region of Congo DR
activities: Group work: Use chart/pictures to classify different types of mineral resources; outline advantages and disadvantages of mining in Liberia
Class discussion: Outline the minerals extracted in Liberia
Field trip to a mining site to see how minerals are extracted
Home work: Draw map of the World and locate major mineral producing countries
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Pictures and diagrams of mining site

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss lumbering with emphasis on the economic values of tree species and provide solutions to lumbering related problems.
objectives: Explain lumbering
Classify the different methods of lumbering based on the type of forest
List the choice trees in lumbering
Outline the factors affecting lumbering in Liberia and elsewhere
Discuss the importance of lumbering
Recommend solutions to the problems facing lumbering in Liberia
contents: Lumbering: definition, methods of exploitation, factors affecting lumbering
Importance of lumbering: provision of jobs, construction, foreign exchange earner
Problems and solutions
Case Studies: Lumbering in Liberia, Lumbering in the Congo Basin of Central Africa, Lumbering in West Africa
activities: Demonstration/Class exercise: Observe pictures/illustrations of lumbering activities and report on methods
Class discussions: Learners provide information on methods of exploitation and factors affecting these
Illustrations: learners identify types and species of plants in lumbering
Field visit to a Saw Mill and write a report
Case study: lumbering in Liberia
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.unit: Human Geography – Secondary and Tertiary Industries
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to categorize industries as well as elaborate on the economic importance of industries in Liberia.
objectives: Categorize secondary industries
List the factors that affect the location of manufacturing industries
Examine the impact of manufacturing industries on the health of communities
Emphasize the role of secondary industries in industrial development
Classify manufacturing industries and briefly describe each class
Discuss the categories of tertiary industries
contents: Manufacturing: definition, classification, major industrial regions
Factors affecting the location of manufacturing industries
Importance of Manufacturing Industries
Problems facing Manufacturing Industries
Trade and Commerce: definition, types (Internal, External)
Transport and Development: definition, means of transport
activities: Field trips and excursions to selected factory/plants in Liberia to observe manufacturing processes and write report
Group discussion on the negative and positive impacts of manufacturing industries
Home work: List factors that influence the location and development of manufacturing industries
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.topic: Sources of Energy, Location and Distribution
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to identify and classify sources of energy, and state their importance to economic development.
objectives: Classify the different sources of energy
Explain the forms of energy
Analyze the factors affecting sources of energy
contents: Sources of Energy classification: Renewable and Non-Renewable
Forms of Energy - Mineral/Fossil Fuels: Coal, Natural Gas, Petroleum, Uranium
Location and Distribution of Power Sources: Hydro-Electric Power (Rivers, Waterfalls, Dams), Solar, Wind, Tidal, Geothermal, Underground, Biomass
Factors affecting sources of energy: Capital, Technology, Climate
Importance of energy
Problems and solutions of energy harnessing
activities: Class discussion: Outline source of energy and state categories/forms
Home work: differentiate between renewable and non-renewable energy; outline factors affecting sources of energy
Field trip to a hydro-electric plant to identify sources of solar and electrical energy
Group work: learners carry out research and report on the application of energy to industrial activities
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Pictures of HEP, waterfall, solar plant, wind mill

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to analyze map information and reshape distances on maps; locate positions on the earth using grid system as well as calculate the gradient of slope.
objectives: Analyze map information
Distinguish various kinds of maps
Apply map scales to reduce, enlarge, and calculate areas and ground distances
Calculate the gradient of slopes
Convert from one scale to another
Discover positions of places using the grid system, or longitudes and latitudes
Detect directions using the compass and bearing
Design diagrams, profiles and sketches resulting from survey exercises
contents: Map: definition, characteristics, types, importance/uses, limitations
Map Scale: definition, types, characteristics of each type
Conversion of Map Scales
activities: Class Discussion: Outline the kinds of map and indicate their characteristics
Home work/class exercise: Draw map to scale, reduce and enlarge maps; practice uses of rulers, protractors; Draw the prismatic compass and illustrate direction and bearing
Group work: survey the school compound or playground and prepare a sketch draft
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to translate statistical information into diagrams and graphs.
objectives: Discuss the sources of statistical data
Design different kinds of graphs based on statistical data
Explain the advantages and disadvantages of the various kinds of graphs
contents: Sources of Statistical Data
The Use of Statistics and Diagrams
Limitations of Statistics
Tabulation of Statistics
Graphical Representation of Statistics
Types: Line graphs, Bar graphs (histograms, simple bar, divergent bar, percentage bar), Circle graphs (pie charts, proportional pie charts, proportional circles)
Advantages and Disadvantages of each type
activities: Group work/Survey: collection and presentation of statistical data on bar and pie charts (boys vs girls)
Home work/assignment: Outline the importance of statistical data in map reading
Class discussion/exercise: State differences among statistics, diagrams and graphs; outline advantages and disadvantages
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.topic: Kinds of Maps and Their Uses
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to analyze map information and convert between map scales.
objectives: Explain map information
Distinguish between the various kinds of maps
Apply map scales to reduce, enlarge, and calculate areas and ground distances
Convert from one map scale to another
contents: Map: definition, characteristics
Types of Maps: Road maps, Weather maps, Atlases, Topographical maps
Importance/Uses of Maps: Give information, Identify landscape, Interpret landscape, Find directions/positions, Find distances
Limitations of Maps
Map Scale: definition, types
activities: Group work: Draw maps to scale, reduce and enlarge maps and scales, practice uses of rulers, protractors and strings
Group discussion: Learners discuss the uses of maps
Field work: Visit to Department of Cartography at Ministry of Lands and Mines & Energy/LISGIS to observe map types and write report
Class exercise: convert from one map scale to another
Home work: Draw linear scale using ruler
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.topic: Finding Positions, Directions and Using Conventional Signs and Symbols
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to locate positions on the earth and recognize conventional signs and symbols.
objectives: Determine positions using lines of latitude and longitude
Apply compass points and angular bearings to show direction
Interpret conventional signs and symbols
Identify the types of signs and symbols
Explain the importance and uses of conventional signs and symbols
Outline the limitations of conventional signs and symbols
contents: Locate positions on maps using latitude and longitude
Locate positions using Grid References
Direction: compass points, angular bearings
Types of North: Magnetic, True, Grid North
Conventional Signs and Symbols: definition, types (Point, Line, Literal, Pictorial, Color symbols)
Importance and Uses of Conventional Signs and Symbols
Limitations in the Use of Signs and Symbols
activities: Class exercise/Assignment: Draw grid map to show eastings and northings; divide learners into two groups and draw sixteen intermediate points of the compass; use compass points and angular bearing to find directions; draw types of conventional signs and symbols
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.topic: Methods of Representing Relief and Simple Contour Landforms
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to explain contour and gradient as well as calculate gradient of slopes.
objectives: Describe contour lines
Distinguish the characteristic features of the different methods of showing relief on maps
Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of the different methods of showing relief
Identify the different contour landforms
Describe gradient
Calculate the gradient of slopes
contents: Methods of Representation of relief: contours, spot height, trigonometrical station, bench mark, hill shading, form lines, hachures
Slope types: concave, convex, steep, gentle, even, uneven
Contour Landforms: Plateau, Ridge, Valley, Spur, Col, Saddle, Pass, Gap, Knoll, Cuesta
Gradient: definition, calculation, vertical exaggeration
Cross sectional drawing/annotated cross profile: framework, alignment
activities: Home work: Draw the different symbols used to show reliefs on maps
Class discussion: learners discuss different types of slopes and methods of calculating gradients
Group work: identify all contour landforms; differentiate relief map from other types
Class exercise: Using profile/cross-section, calculate the vertical exaggeration
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.unit: Practical and Regional Geography – Map Reading
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to discuss the sources of statistical data and design different kinds of graphs.
objectives: Discuss the sources of statistical data
Design different kinds of graphs based on statistical data
Explain the advantages and disadvantages of the various kinds of graphs
contents: Sources of Statistical Data
The Use of Statistics and Diagrams
Limitations of Statistics
Tabulation of Statistics
Graphical Representation of Statistics: choice of scale, use of squared-paper

Types of graphs: Line graphs (simple, combined, compound), Bar graphs (histograms, simple bar, divergent bar, percentage bar), Circle graphs (pie charts, proportional pie charts, proportional circles)
activities: Group discussion: learners discuss the difference between statistical diagrams and statistical maps and their uses; discuss advantages and disadvantages of graphs
Class work: Draw the types of graphs on poster sheets
Assignment: Group presentation on the sources of statistical data
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.unit: Practical and Regional Geography – Map Reading
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to classify drainage patterns and explain the influence it has on settlement and communication.
objectives: Describe drainage and drainage systems
Identify drainage patterns
Explain the influence of drainage on settlement and communication
Describe drainage basin
contents: Drainage: definition
Drainage Systems: inland drainage, river capture
Types of Drainage Patterns: Dendritic, Trellis, Radial
River Profile: Upper course, Middle course, Lower course
Influence of Drainage on Settlement
Influence of Drainage on Communication
activities: Group discussion: learners discuss drainage and its systems; distinguish between natural and artificial drainage; state the economic importance
Home work/Class work: Learners draw a diagram of the different drainage patterns
Brainstorm: Learners relate drainage to activities in settlements
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.unit: Practical and Regional Geography – Map Reading
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to demonstrate skills and techniques to conduct survey.
objectives: Identify the instruments used by surveyors in surveying
Demonstrate skills and techniques of survey on the field
Design diagrams, profiles and sketches resulting from survey exercises
Identify obstacles in ranging and chaining
Explain the different methods of survey
Outline the advantages and disadvantages of the different survey methods
contents: Definition of Survey
Description and uses of Chain Survey Equipment
Types of Traverse surveying: Closed traverse, Open traverse
Methods of Traverse surveying
Advantages and disadvantages of survey methods
activities: Group discussion: Discuss the different survey instruments and their uses; State advantages/disadvantages of different methods of survey
Group exercise on survey field work and prepare chart from information gathered
Assignment: Learners draw a diagram of the different drainage patterns
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Global Position System or their pictures

topics.unit: Practical and Regional Geography – Map Reading
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to elaborate on the Geographic Information System.
objectives: Explain Geographic Information System (GIS)
Outline the sources of geographical data
Discuss the importance of geographical data and the GIS
Examine the components of GIS
Describe the procedure of GIS
Explain the relationship between GIS and remote sensing
contents: Meaning of Geographic Information System (GIS)
Geographic Data
Sources of GIS
Importance of Geographic Data and The GIS
Components of GIS: Hardware and Software
Procedures
Satellite Remote Sensing: definition of concepts
GIS Implementation
activities: Class discussion/Exercise/Assignment: Group learners to discuss GIS
Name the components of GIS hardware and software and the procedures of GIS
Learners explain the relationship between GIS and Remote Sensing
Learners outline the importance of GIS as a source of geographic information
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Computer system with GIS software installed

topics.unit: Human and Regional Geography of Liberia and Africa
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to describe Liberia in terms of vegetation, size, soil types and settlement.
objectives: Discuss climate and the factors that affect climate
List the vegetation zones of Liberia and factors that affect vegetation
Classify soil types of Liberia
Outline the causes of soil erosion
Compare the settlement pattern of Liberia
Describe the five geographical regions of Liberia
Outline the location and size of Liberia
contents: Location, Position and Size of Liberia
Relief of Liberia
Drainage and lakes
Climate of Liberia
Natural Vegetation of Liberia
Soils of Liberia: soil types, soil erosion
Population and Settlement
activities: Class work/Exercise: learners discuss the location, position and size of Liberia using appropriate map; outline and discuss the natural features of Liberia; discuss the size, distribution and majority of the population of Liberia
Assignment: Learners research and report on how erosion affects population and settlement in Liberia
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Political, relief, drainage, vegetation, demographic, land use maps

topics.unit: Human and Regional Geography of Africa
topics.topic: West, East, North, South and Central Africa
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to outline the geopolitical regions of Africa, identify the territorial boundaries and distinguish the natural features.
objectives: Discuss the countries, size and the political boundaries of the various regions of Africa
Examine the physical features and natural resources of the various African regions
Discuss trade and communications relative to the population of the various regions
Analyze factors influencing climate and agricultural activities of Africa
contents: The Geography of West Africa
Geography of East Africa
Geography of Equatorial and Central Africa
The Geography of North Africa
The Geography of Southern Africa
activities: Class Discussion/Exercise: learners discuss the regions of Africa, the countries, size, and population with appropriate maps; outline and discuss natural features of the various African regions
Group assignment: Learners research and report on how trade, agriculture and communication technology influence economic development of the various African regions
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Vegetation, drainage, demographic, land use, climatic maps
Pictures showing manufacturing industries, cities, mining sites and major sea ports

topics.unit: Human and Regional Geography of Liberia
topics.topic: Primary and Tertiary Industries of Liberia
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to analyze the Liberian economy and recommend solutions to improve the economic system.
objectives: Differentiate the primary, secondary, and tertiary sectors of the economy of Liberia
Discuss the importance of each sector to the economic and infrastructural development of Liberia
Debate the problems hampering the growth and development of Liberia's economy
Recommend ways to improve the primary, secondary and tertiary industries of Liberia
contents: Agriculture: Case Study of Rubber plantation in Liberia (Firestone Operations), current trend and prospects
Lumbering
Fishing
Trade and Commerce
Transport and Development
Tourism
activities: Class discussion: learners discuss the three sectors of the Liberian economy
Group assignment: Group report on problems affecting economic growth and recommend possible solutions
Field trip/excursion: visit to industrial sites to observe activities and problems associated with production processes
Individual assignment: Learners research and report on the different industries and their production processes
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Documentaries on commercial activities, exports and imports
Picture of beaches and holiday resort

topics.unit: Human and Regional Geography – World Population and Settlement
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to describe world population in relation to distribution and movements; discuss settlement patterns and the consequences of migration; adopt family planning as a means of population control.
objectives: Explain the concept of world population
Describe population distribution patterns of the world
Distinguish between overpopulation and under population
Outline factors which influence population movements
Discuss why and how population census is conducted
Identify the types of settlements and settlement patterns
Explain the consequences of migration at the source and receiving regions
Delineate family planning and empowerment
contents: World Population: definition, distribution, factors affecting growth, terms associated with population growth
Settlement: definition, types, factors affecting settlement growth, settlement patterns and functions
Population control and family planning
activities: Home work: Draw population maps indicating densely and sparsely populated regions
Class Debate: Hold debate to discuss population control in relation to family planning and women empowerment
Class work: List some overpopulated and underpopulated countries
Field trip/exercise: to identify types of settlements, over and under-populated regions of Liberia
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
Pictures showing densely and sparsely populated areas

topics.outcomes: Learners are able to relate climate and vegetation as well as analyze climatic data.
objectives: Differentiate between climate and vegetation
List the elements of weather and climate
Analyze climatic data using charts
Discuss the different types of natural vegetation
Examine the factors that affect vegetation
contents: Define climate and name its elements
Differences between weather and climate
Elements of weather and climate
Preparation of climatic chart
Natural Vegetation: definition
Factors affecting the development of vegetation
Case Study: Liberia (Forest, Mountain, Savanna, Mangrove Swamps and Marshlands)
activities: Class discussion: the causes of weather and climate changes; importance of climate and vegetation
Group work: Group presentation on the weather instruments and their uses
Class work/exercise: Prepare a climatic chart and calculate mean annual temperature, mean monthly temperature, range of rainfall and temperature
Assignment: Learners research and report on deforestation, afforestation and reforestation
Field trip: learners move outdoor to feel and observe changes in weather condition
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1

topics.topic: General Revision – Map Reading, Industries of Liberia, Climate and Vegetation, Regional Geography of Africa, Population and Settlement
topics.outcomes: Learners are able to demonstrate understanding of Geographic related topics.
objectives: Recap and remember the definitions, elements, description and activities of the geographic topics covered, and prepare for school as well as WASSCE exams
contents: Map Reading
Types of industries of Liberia
Climate and natural vegetation
Regional Geography of Africa
Population and Settlement
activities: Class discussion
Group presentation
Individual Project
Facilitator provides clarity on questions learners may post
materials: Abegunde et al. Senior Secondary Geography Bk. 1
